description:
You are assisting a biomedical researcher who is cleaning up their project directory.  
The goal is to move two existing CSV datasets from the “staging” area into a new,
properly named project folder and to leave behind a **machine-readable log file**
documenting exactly what was moved and where it went.  
All work must be done within your own home directory so no elevated privileges are
required.

Initial layout (already present before you start):

/home/user/raw_data/avian_flu_cases.csv  
/home/user/raw_data/avian_flu_metadata.csv  

Desired final layout:

/home/user/datasets/avian_flu/avian_flu_cases.csv  
/home/user/datasets/avian_flu/avian_flu_metadata.csv  
/home/user/datasets/avian_flu/organize.log

Your tasks:

1. Create the directory /home/user/datasets/avian_flu.  
   • Create any missing parent directories on the way.  

2. Move the two CSV files listed above from /home/user/raw_data/ into
   the newly created /home/user/datasets/avian_flu/ directory,
   preserving their original filenames.  
   • After the operation, /home/user/raw_data/ **must no longer contain**
     avian_flu_cases.csv or avian_flu_metadata.csv.

3. Generate a plain-text log file named organize.log inside
   /home/user/datasets/avian_flu/.  
   The file must contain exactly three comma-separated lines **with no extra blank
   lines or spaces**:

   Line 1 (header):  
   FILE,OLD_PATH,NEW_PATH

   Line 2 (data for cases file):  
   avian_flu_cases.csv,/home/user/raw_data/avian_flu_cases.csv,/home/user/datasets/avian_flu/avian_flu_cases.csv

   Line 3 (data for metadata file):  
   avian_flu_metadata.csv,/home/user/raw_data/avian_flu_metadata.csv,/home/user/datasets/avian_flu/avian_flu_metadata.csv

The automated checker will validate:

• Existence of directory /home/user/datasets/avian_flu  
• Presence (and correct filename) of the two CSV files inside that directory  
• Absence of those CSV files in /home/user/raw_data/  
• Existence of /home/user/datasets/avian_flu/organize.log with **exactly** the
  three comma-separated lines shown above (including header order, case, and
  absolute paths).

No other output or files will be examined.  
Hint: this can be completed with only 2–3 shell commands.
truth:
Pre-existing files (already placed before the agent starts):

1. Path: /home/user/raw_data/avian_flu_cases.csv  
   Contents (one line): id,country,date,cases

2. Path: /home/user/raw_data/avian_flu_metadata.csv  
   Contents (one line): source,contact,email

Required final state for verification:

1. /home/user/datasets/avian_flu directory exists.  
2. /home/user/datasets/avian_flu/avian_flu_cases.csv exists and its content is
   unchanged (id,country,date,cases).  
3. /home/user/datasets/avian_flu/avian_flu_metadata.csv exists and its content
   is unchanged (source,contact,email).  
4. /home/user/raw_data directory still exists but **does not** contain the two
   CSV files listed above.  
5. /home/user/datasets/avian_flu/organize.log exists and contains **exactly**:

FILE,OLD_PATH,NEW_PATH
avian_flu_cases.csv,/home/user/raw_data/avian_flu_cases.csv,/home/user/datasets/avian_flu/avian_flu_cases.csv
avian_flu_metadata.csv,/home/user/raw_data/avian_flu_metadata.csv,/home/user/datasets/avian_flu/avian_flu_metadata.csv

(no trailing blank lines, spaces, or additional text).
name: task_000000_49abb02c
